patch.value: The release workflow currently publishes npm before trying to persist its version and changelog commit. Protected main rejects that direct push, so npm can advance while GitHub main/tag/release remain incomplete. The workflow must create or reuse a deterministic release PR, merge it through normal branch protection, verify the merged version, then publish and tag the exact merged commit. Retries must recover cleanly from partial runs without choosing a new version or duplicating publication.
Merge release metadata before npm publication

Daily Release run 29274764877 published pm-changelog@2026.7.13 and then failed because its direct HEAD:main push was rejected by protected-branch policy. Replace the direct push with a release-branch pull-request transaction that merges validated metadata before any irreversible npm publication.

Workflow requests pull-requests:write and never directly pushes HEAD:main; release metadata is pushed to a deterministic branch and merged via a PR before npm publish; merged main is verified at the intended npm version; tag is created on the merged commit only after publish succeeds; retry paths reuse an existing PR/version and skip already-published npm versions; workflow syntax, full release gate, and TypeScript workflow-contract tests pass.
Failure reproduced from GitHub logs; branch protection confirmed; npm and tag state inspected; no duplicate pm item found.
Restores trustworthy, recoverable daily releases without weakening main protection.
Prevents npm/GitHub split-brain releases across all future pm-changelog versions.
Daily Release works with protected main using only free GitHub features and the repository GITHUB_TOKEN.
The latest manual Daily Release failed after publication and the workflow remains broken for every future change.
GitHub Actions Daily Release run 29274764877 on protected main
Dispatch Daily Release from main with changes since the latest tag; observe npm publish succeed and git push origin HEAD:main fail with GH006.
Validated release metadata merges through a PR before npm publication; the release tag points at the merged main commit and retries are idempotent.
npm publication occurs first, then protected main rejects the release commit, producing split-brain release state.
Automated releases can publish artifacts that are not represented by main, a release tag, or GitHub release notes.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Evidence: run 29274764877 failed at Push release tag and main with GH006 after the npm publish step; repository protection requires pull requests and conversation resolution.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Do not disable or bypass branch protection. Move the irreversible publish after a normal PR merge and exact merged-commit verification.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Release workflows should persist and validate release metadata before publishing immutable registry artifacts; retries can then converge safely.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Official GitHub documentation verification: the pull-request merge endpoint accepts an expected head sha and returns the base SHA after a rebase merge. GITHUB_TOKEN-created PR events may create approval-required workflow runs, so the release transaction does not rely on that duplicate run: it executes the full release gate before PR creation and again on the exact merged SHA before npm publication.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Repository Actions permissions audit: default_workflow_permissions is read and can_approve_pull_request_reviews is false. GitHub documents that the latter repository setting controls whether GITHUB_TOKEN can create/approve PRs. Enable it after the reviewed workflow merges; retain read-only defaults and workflow-local contents/id-token/pull-requests scopes.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Linked-test correction: the first pm test run used plain npm run release:check, so npm read the host user config and failed on its legacy allow-scripts entry after all 139 tests passed. This is not a pm CLI defect. Replaced the link with NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG=/dev/null npm run release:check, matching the documented hermetic gate.

author: codex-root

patch.value: Actionlint passes both workflows; four TypeScript transaction-contract tests pass; the pm-linked hermetic release gate passes 139/139 tests, production audit with zero vulnerabilities, package dry run, and changelog verification. Official GitHub token, merge API, and repository-setting requirements are documented.

Replaced the direct protected-main push with a deterministic release-branch PR transaction. The workflow exact-SHA merges validated metadata before npm publication, rechecks the full gate on the merged main commit, and tags only that verified commit after publishing.

message: Record protected release transaction implementation and verification

author: codex-root

patch.value: Direct main push removed; exact-SHA PR merge, merged-main release gate, idempotent publish, and exact-commit tag are enforced by workflow and TypeScript tests.
Release metadata reaches protected main before registry publication.
Protected-branch-compatible release PR transaction implemented and verified.
Implementation and all local acceptance gates complete; live workflow dispatch follows reviewed merge and will reopen on any production discrepancy.

message: Close after actionlint and pm-linked release gate

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Post-closure retry hardening: an interrupted PR merge now resumes the untagged package version even after a calendar-day rollover. If npm/tag succeeded but GitHub release creation failed, the next no-change run verifies the registry artifact and reconstructs the missing release. Actionlint and five TypeScript workflow contract tests pass.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Pre-push Greptile review 112815e5-d3f6-47be-8c39-317a28a9d5c6 findings accepted. The prior pmc-ermk no-provenance fallback is superseded: three failed attested publishes now fail safely and rely on the same-version retry transaction. Step-order tests now anchor exact six-space YAML step names to prevent prefix matches. Both changes have direct TypeScript contract coverage.

author: codex-root

value.text: Greptile follow-up 9c8f3b24-f068-464c-88e9-a8eae0ea126b returned 5/5 with no comments. Its non-blocking stale-branch observation was still implemented: the metadata-already-on-main resume path now deletes any deterministic release branch before continuing, with a TypeScript contract assertion.

value.text: PR #68 bot review: accepted Sourcery's exact changelog-tag regex escape, flexible exact-name step lookup, and retry-exhaustion assertion; accepted CodeRabbit's job-scoped pull-request permission and env-safe release-tag handling. CodeRabbit confirmed concurrency is already present. Gemini was quota-limited. All accepted findings are covered by the release workflow contract tests.
